content.claim: Hungarian birth cohorts have contracted sharply: fertility rate fell from 1.6 in 2021 to 1.3 by 2026 (Q1 2026 at 1.29); birth count has fallen approximately 20% in four years, with 17,000 fewer one-year-olds than four-year-olds, translating to 600-800 fewer kindergarten cohorts nationally.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Demography lens
limitations: Recent fertility data (Q1 2026 at 1.29) appears to be from preliminary reporting; official statistics not directly linked
Regional variance is noted as pronounced (20%+ declines in some counties) but no county-level projections provided
Projection from births to school closures (600-800 kindergarten groups to 'hundred small schools worth') uses rough equivalency without detailed capacity assumptions
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
